body: {"rationale":"Bead scores 3/8 on the authoring rubric. Passing: parent+deps explicit (ddx-e34994e2 + 4 deps listed), standalone description (no /tmp refs, no chat references). Failing or partial on six criteria: (1) Title is a noun phrase, not an imperative verb — 'specs:' is a scope tag, not a command. (2) No file:line references anywhere — agent cannot identify which files hold FEAT-006/010/014 or where ADR-021 should land. (3) AC has numbers but zero verifiable test names or go test -run filters. (4) No wired-in assertion. (5) Neither 'cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test' nor 'lefthook run pre-commit passes' appear in AC — even for a kind:doc bead lefthook must gate the attempt. (6) Labels carry phase/story/area/kind but no cross-refs to the existing ADRs or specs being amended (e.g. spec:FEAT-006, adr:ADR-021). AC line 3 ('ddx doc audit clean') is not a real command and cannot be reproduced by a sub-agent cold.","score":3,"suggested_fixes":["Title: use imperative verb — e.g. 'Update FEAT-006/010/014 specs and author ADR-021 documenting escalation-ladder design'","Description: add explicit file paths for each artifact the agent must touch, e.g. 'docs/helix/specs/FEAT-006.md:L42', 'docs/helix/specs/FEAT-010.md', 'docs/helix/specs/FEAT-014.md', 'docs/helix/adr/ADR-021.md (new)'","Description: add a PROBLEM statement — what observable gap or inconsistency made this work necessary?","AC: rewrite each line as a verifiable check with a concrete command or grep pattern, e.g. 'grep -q MinPower docs/helix/adr/ADR-021.md exits 0', 'grep -q CorrelationID docs/helix/specs/FEAT-010.md exits 0'","AC: add 'lefthook run pre-commit passes' as the final AC line; add 'cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./... green' or explicitly waive with rationale if no Go files are touched","Labels: add cross-refs — e.g. 'spec:FEAT-006', 'spec:FEAT-010', 'spec:FEAT-014', 'adr:ADR-021' so reviewers and tooling can trace lineage"],"waivers_applied":[]}

verification_command=ddx doc audit
exit_code=0
output:
Document graph is clean.

verification_command passed: ddx doc audit
rationale: status: already_complete
verification_command: ddx doc audit

All three ACs are satisfied by content already present in the codebase at the
base revision (e338ce9e3). A prior execution of this bead authored the required
artifacts and had them merged into main; the bead was never formally closed.

AC 1 — All three FEATs updated:
  FEAT-006 §"Review routing request facts" (lines 78-84): reviewer pairing via
    MinPower bump + role=reviewer + correlation metadata; references ADR-024.
  FEAT-006 §"Escalation And Review Routing Boundary" (lines 213-226):
    MinPower/MaxPower escalation ladder with no Fizeau extension required;
    DDx does not translate the floor into a concrete model/provider.
  FEAT-010 §"Agent Power and Retry": escalation via MinPower raise, sticky
    passthrough constraints, capability-sensitive retry eligibility.
  FEAT-010 §"Cost stop" (step 8 of the decision tree): implementation and
    review attempts both count against the drain-level cost cap.
  FEAT-014 requirement 13a: review cost and pairing signals normalized with
    role=reviewer, review_group_id, reviewer slot, implementer/reviewer route
    facts; review-pairing-degraded is a routing-quality signal for operators,
    not a route-selection input owned by DDx.
  FEAT-014 §"Out of Scope": queue-drain budget stop and reviewer-cost cap are
    owned by FEAT-010 and ADR-024; FEAT-014 supplies the normalized cost signals.

AC 2 — ADR-021 authored:
  docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-024-power-escalation-and-review-routing.md
  exists and was authored by this bead. The bead title used "ADR-021" as a
  working number; ADR-024 was assigned because ADRs 021-023 were already
  occupied. ADR-024 explicitly documents:
    - "These fields are Day-1 observability."
    - "Future agent-side routing intelligence may use Role and correlation
       internally, but DDx does not specify that algorithm and must not
       depend on it."
  Both the Day-1 observability framing and the Fizeau-internal evolution
  clause are present verbatim.

AC 3 — ddx doc audit clean:
  Verified: `ddx doc audit` returns "Document graph is clean."
